rationale.root_cause: They pinpointed the unsafe strcpy at line 9 copying argv data (argc[1]) into buf[256], noting untrusted/user-supplied input can exceed the buffer and naming it a stack-buffer-overflow. This meets the requirement to localize and classify the defect.
rationale.local_fix: They suggested strncpy/snprintf with null-termination but did not reject overlong input before copying, which the rubric explicitly requires. Their fixes would truncate rather than refuse oversized input.
rationale.global_fix: They proposed eliminating raw, unbounded string handling by using dynamic allocation, bounded string APIs, and centralized safe wrappers/validation. This satisfies the requirement to remove unbounded copies from the data path.
